steps.title: Eliminate choices with incorrect y-intercepts
steps.content: The line crosses the y-axis at approximately $n = 16,000$ when $t = 0$. This is the y-intercept. Choices B and D have y-intercepts of approximately 4,690 and 9,060, which don't match the graph. Only choices A and C have y-intercepts close to 16,090.

steps.title: Use slope to select between A and C
steps.content:
Choice A has slope 4,680; Choice C has slope 9,060. To determine which is correct, calculate the slope using two clear points on the line.

Using $(0, 16,000)$ and $(10, 64,000)$: $$m = \frac{64,000 - 16,000}{10} = 4,800$$
Since 4,800 is much closer to 4,680 than to 9,060, the equation with slope 4,680 matches the line shown.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
